svg.text: 8 capabilities · 24 apps · one app per capability is the deep exemplar (★); others exercise it more lightly

g.text: deep exemplar — exercises it most heavily
Agent emits a tool call · sees observation ·
decides next action · repeats until done.
13 MCP calls per question on the trace shown.
Up to 2 retry rounds on validation failure.
Pick the right tool from a small toolkit
based on what the question needs.
to the agent. LLM picks per turn.
Accept files of different types; agent
dispatches to the right extractor in-loop.
Output must cite which source produced
runs it against a known oracle.
Only app in the suite that does this.
falling back to a single-tool sequence.
guards push back; agent renames + retries.
What this matrix tells you about the suite
Caps 1–2 are universal — every app loops + picks tools. That's the baseline "does CUGA work" check; table stakes.
Caps 3–6 (web · multi-modal · RAG · citations) split the apps into the substantive groups: research-flavored, document-flavored, media-flavored.
Caps 7–8 (code synthesis + self-correction) are the deepest agent stress-test — and only bird_invocable_api exercises them. Single biggest IP in the suite.

svg.text: If a future evaluator asks "what does this suite actually exercise about an agent?" — this matrix is the answer. Each cap is reproducible, measurable, and testable in isolation.
